title: Cloudflare’s new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers’ content
slides: Cloudflare just flipped the table on AI companies.
Its new policy blocks crawlers unless publishers are paid.
Even Google and OpenAI now face paywalls for scraping.
Your content becomes a licensed asset, not free data.
Audit your site's crawler rules before next month.
thread_text:
1. They flipped the table

Cloudflare just blocked all AI crawlers unless publishers get paid. Google and OpenAI are now behind a paywall.

2. What changed overnight

Your blog posts are now licensed assets. The era of free data for AI training just ended.

3. The real target

This isn't about small sites. It's a direct hit on Google's knowledge graph and OpenAI's training pipeline.

4. Who benefits most

Publishers with high-traffic archives just gained leverage. Small creators might get squeezed in negotiations.

5. Check your robots.txt

Open your site's crawler rules today. If you haven't set payment terms, do it before next month.
video_script: Cloudflare just blocked AI crawlers unless paid.
Google and OpenAI now face paywalls for scraping.
Your content becomes a licensed asset now.
Audit your site's crawler rules immediately.
This shifts power to publishers with archives.

Cloudflare just blocked all AI crawlers unless publishers get paid — Google and OpenAI are now behind a paywall for scraping your content. ⚡

Your blog posts just became licensed assets. The era of free data for AI training ended overnight.

Publishers with high-traffic archives just gained leverage — but small creators might get squeezed in negotiations.

Open your site's robots.txt today. If you haven't set payment terms, do it before next month. 🔍
